questions.q: To which tribal community did Birsa Munda belong?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda was a leader of the Munda tribal community of the Chhotanagpur region.

questions.q: By what revered title was Birsa Munda known among his people?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda was revered as Dharti Aaba, meaning 'Father of the Earth'.

questions.q: What name is given to the uprising that Birsa Munda led?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda led the Ulgulan, meaning 'The Great Tumult', against colonial rule and exploitation.

questions.q: In which region did Birsa Munda's movement take place?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda's uprising was centred in the Chhotanagpur plateau region of eastern India.

questions.q: In which year did Birsa Munda die?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda died in jail in 1900 at a very young age.

questions.q: What did Birsa Munda's movement primarily protest against?
questions.explanation: His Ulgulan rebellion opposed colonial land policies and the exploitation of tribal communities.

questions.q: Birsa Munda is remembered chiefly as a leader of which kind of resistance?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda is honoured as a pioneering leader of tribal resistance against colonial rule.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Birsa Munda when he died?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda died at only around 25 years of age, making him one of the youngest icons of the freedom struggle.

questions.q: Where did Birsa Munda die in 1900?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda died in jail in 1900 while in British custody.

questions.q: Birsa Munda's uprising occurred chiefly during which decade?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda led his movement against colonial exploitation during the 1890s.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the heartland of Birsa Munda's movement located?
questions.explanation: The Chhotanagpur region of his uprising lies in present-day Jharkhand.

questions.q: The word 'Ulgulan' associated with Birsa Munda translates roughly to what?
questions.explanation: Ulgulan means 'The Great Tumult' or 'Great Uproar' in the Mundari language.

questions.q: Birsa Munda also founded a religious-reform movement known by what name?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda founded the Birsait faith, a reform movement urging his followers toward monotheism and self-respect.

questions.q: Against which exploitative class of landlords and moneylenders did the Mundas rebel?
questions.explanation: The Mundas rebelled against the 'dikus', the outsiders—landlords, moneylenders and contractors—who dispossessed tribal land.

questions.q: Which traditional tribal land-tenure system was being eroded by colonial policy, fuelling the uprising?
questions.explanation: The khuntkatti communal land system of the Mundas was being undermined by colonial and landlord encroachment, a key grievance behind the revolt.

questions.q: In which year was Birsa Munda born?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda was born in 1875 in the Chhotanagpur region.

questions.q: Birsa Munda's followers regarded him not only as a leader but also as what?
questions.explanation: Birsa's followers viewed him as a prophet and healer with divine powers, calling him Bhagwan as well as Dharti Aaba.

questions.q: What goal did Birsa Munda proclaim regarding rule over the Munda lands?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda called for the establishment of 'Munda Raj', tribal self-rule free of dikus and colonial control.

questions.q: The Munda uprising reached its peak in armed confrontation around which years?
questions.explanation: The armed phase of Birsa Munda's Ulgulan climaxed in the turn of 1899 to 1900.

questions.q: Birsa Munda's struggle is often cited as a precursor to which later legislation protecting tribal land?
questions.explanation: The agitation contributed to the passage of the Chotanagpur Tenancy Act of 1908, which protected tribal land rights.

questions.q: In his early life Birsa Munda was briefly exposed to which religion through mission schooling?
questions.explanation: Birsa attended a Christian mission school in his youth, an experience that later shaped his own reformist religious teachings.

questions.q: The day of Birsa Munda's birth is now observed in India as which national day?
questions.explanation: His birth anniversary, 15 November, is observed as Janjatiya Gaurav Divas, or Tribal Pride Day, in his honour.

questions.q: Birsa Munda was first arrested in 1895 partly for stirring unrest over what issue?
questions.explanation: His 1895 arrest followed agitation over tribal forest and land rights against colonial encroachment.

questions.q: A major armed clash of the Ulgulan took place at which hill in January 1900?
questions.explanation: British forces fired on Birsa's followers gathered at Dombari Hill (Dombari Buru) in January 1900, killing many tribals.

questions.q: What was the officially recorded cause of Birsa Munda's death in jail?
questions.explanation: The British recorded that Birsa Munda died of cholera in Ranchi jail in June 1900, though the circumstances have been questioned.

questions.q: In which jail did Birsa Munda die in 1900?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda died while imprisoned in Ranchi jail in the Chhotanagpur region.

questions.q: Birsa Munda urged his followers to abandon which practices as part of his reform message?
questions.explanation: His Birsait teachings called on followers to give up animal sacrifice, alcohol and certain superstitions in favour of a purified faith.

questions.q: After his capture in early 1900, Birsa Munda was betrayed and seized in which kind of location?
options: A forest hideout in the Jaikaran hills
questions.explanation: Birsa was captured in February 1900 from a forest hideout in the Jaikaran hills after being betrayed by informers.

questions.q: In 2021 the Indian government inaugurated a museum dedicated to Birsa Munda and tribal freedom fighters where?
questions.explanation: A tribal freedom-fighters museum honouring Birsa Munda was opened at Ranchi, near the site of his imprisonment.

questions.q: Birsa Munda is one of the few tribal leaders whose portrait hangs in which national institution?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda's portrait hangs in the Central Hall of the Indian Parliament, a rare honour for a tribal leader.
